name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI workflow platform, orchestrating IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance workflows for over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.671B grew 22% YoY, with 2026 full-year guidance of $15.74-15.78B. The company has 630 customers with $5M+ ACV, up 22% YoY. Market cap approximately $103B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to an enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to an enterprise AI platform (2020-present), expanding TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ — a successful multi-phase business model pivot that redefined the category [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): AI agents are shipping to customers with measurable impact — the company achieved 22% revenue growth with headcount-neutral staffing, automated 90% of customer service use cases, and CEO McDermott states AI will boost productivity enough that ServiceNow won't backfill open positions [2]. Market Share (1/2): Category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration with no close independent competitor; 85% of Fortune 500 as customers and 630 customers at $5M+ ACV demonstrates dominant positioning [1]. Product Pipeline (2/2): 16 transactions over $5M in net new ACV in Q1 2026 (nearly 80% YoY growth), Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected H2 2026, and agentic AI features with specific customer adoption metrics provide clear pipeline visibility [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D investment, while substantial in absolute terms, is approximately sector-average for enterprise SaaS.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, thoughtful, and connects the company's work to removing enterprise friction so humans can focus on higher-value work [2]. The mission differentiates from generic technology statements and shapes product development decisions. CEO McDermott consistently articulates this vision across multiple economic cycles. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits, the company has achieved carbon neutrality and 100% renewable electricity, and runs diversity and pay equity programs [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): Named to Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For and Glassdoor Best Places to Work; employee reviews describe transparent leadership, progressive culture, and CEO energy as differentiators [2]. CEO approval ratings consistently above 90%. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares during the January 2026 software sector selloff and extended his contract to 2030 — significant open-market buying at personal financial risk [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 delivered subscription revenue of $3.671B (up 22% YoY) and total revenue of $3.770B (up 22%), with 2026 guidance of $15.74-15.78B representing 22-22.5% growth [1]. D/E of 20.7% scores 1.0 (well below 70% target). ROE of 16.1% scores 1.0 (above 15% target). Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% scores 1.0. Revenue 3-year CAGR of 22.4% scores 1.0. Forward P/E of 19.9 scores 1.0 (at target of 20). Profit margin of 12.6% scores 0.629. PEG ratio of 0.97 scores 1.0 (at target of 1.0). Weighted sum 9.63 / 10 max weight = 9.6, rounds to 10. Liquidity strongly passes: 24.0M avg volume x $99.92 = $2.4B [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less — CEO McDermott argues 'AI excels at identifying problems but cannot execute solutions' and ServiceNow controls the execution layer [2]. The company orchestrates billions of enterprise workflows annually, and AI agents need orchestration infrastructure to function. However, the stock has slumped along with other software names as agentic AI threatens to disrupt the industry's business model — if AI agents can autonomously complete workflows end-to-end, the orchestration layer becomes less valuable. Analysts have flagged this as a genuine EXPOSED risk for the sector. The company's 22% growth with headcount-neutral staffing demonstrates AI efficiency but also validates the displacement thesis. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow generates quantifiable AI revenue through AI-powered workflow products, AI agent features, and the Moveworks acquisition contributing measurable subscription revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott is a rare enterprise software CEO who operates with explicit long-term platform thinking. His contract extension to 2030, $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 selloff, and consistent articulation of the 'make work work better' mission reflect genuine infinite game commitment. The platform pivot from ITSM to enterprise AI demonstrates existential flexibility. However, the company's reliance on one charismatic CEO creates succession risk.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites both technology partners and customers to contribute. McDermott's consistent articulation across multiple economic cycles earns 2 points. However, the cause is relatively young (post-McDermott arrival in 2019) and hasn't been tested through a true existential crisis.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): ServiceNow consistently ranks in top tier for enterprise software on Glassdoor and Fortune Best Companies lists, with CEO approval ratings above 90% [2]. The company's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creates psychological safety. However, McDermott's statement that AI means ServiceNow won't need to backfill open positions creates tension with the trust narrative, and 29,187 employees create variability in cultural experience.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose integrations push ServiceNow to develop better connectors and expand platform capabilities [2]. The acquisitions of Moveworks and Armis reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing ServiceNow's portfolio gaps and motivating expansion rather than defensive actions.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The three-phase pivot from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility [1]. Each transformation built on the previous while expanding TAM 10x. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly — it redefined the category around AI-powered workflow orchestration.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout — when the stock dropped on AI disruption fears — is a clear example of acting in service of the cause at personal financial cost [2]. The headcount-neutral growth strategy demonstrates willingness to reorganize around AI efficiency. However, these actions are reinforced by strong financial performance rather than truly sacrificing short-term results.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.671B (up 22% YoY) with 2026 guidance of $15.74-15.78B representing consistent 22%+ growth, 16 transactions over $5M net new ACV (nearly 80% YoY growth), and 630 customers at $5M+ ACV [1]. The forward P/E of 19.9x and PEG of 0.97x represent remarkable value for a company delivering 22% revenue growth with improving profitability. CEO McDermott's $3M personal share purchase and 2030 contract extension signal maximum conviction. The enterprise AI workflow market is expanding, and ServiceNow's platform position makes it the connective tissue between AI agents and enterprise systems [2].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on AI disruption risk: ServiceNow's stock has slumped in 2026 as markets reassess whether agentic AI will complement or replace enterprise workflow platforms [2]. If AI agents mature to execute workflows autonomously end-to-end, the orchestration layer becomes less valuable — and ServiceNow's own demonstration of headcount-neutral 22% growth validates the displacement thesis. The trailing P/E of 59.5x and profit margin of only 12.6% mean the stock has limited margin of safety; any guidance miss would trigger severe multiple compression. McDermott's warning that AI could drive 30% unemployment among new graduates reflects a broader disruption dynamic that could eventually affect ServiceNow's own value proposition [2]. The company's reliance on one charismatic CEO creates key-person risk.
key_catalysts: Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, adding security operations TAM and expanding the platform's enterprise footprint
AI agent adoption metrics materializing in Q2-Q3 2026 results with specific customer automation data and net new ACV attribution to AI features
Profit margin expansion toward 15-20% as AI-driven operational efficiency improves the cost structure while 22%+ revenue growth continues
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration within 2-3 years, faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates, compressing ServiceNow's value proposition
Trailing P/E of 59.5x and 12.6% profit margin create valuation fragility — any quarterly guidance miss or growth deceleration could trigger 20%+ multiple compression
CEO key-person risk: McDermott's charisma and vision are deeply embedded in the company's narrative and investor confidence — his departure would create significant uncertainty
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Earnings Release — subscription revenue $3.671B, 2026 guidance $15.74-15.78B, SEC Form 8-K, April 2026
ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott — AI strategy, $3M share purchase, headcount-neutral growth, CNBC and Fast Company, 2026
ServiceNow ESG and Corporate Responsibility — carbon neutrality, Fortune Best Companies, Glassdoor recognition, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed May 2026
